exampleSentences: Mỗi dịp Tết, gia đình em thường về quê thăm bà con.
Bà con trong làng luôn giúp đỡ nhau khi gặp khó khăn.
Anh ấy rất quý mến bà con nên thường xuyên gọi điện hỏi thăm.
notes_md:
**bà con** = relatives, extended family members (not just your parents or siblings).  

It’s a collective noun, usually uncountable — you don’t say numbers directly with it. If you want to be specific, use **họ hàng** for “relatives” in a more formal or neutral way.  

It can also mean “folks” or “everyone” when talking warmly to a group, especially in villages or public speeches — like addressing the community.  

So depending on context, it’s either your extended family or the wider community around you.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Every Tet holiday, my family usually returns to the hometown to visit relatives.
Relatives in the village always help each other when facing difficulties.
He cares a lot about relatives so he often calls to check in.
